“ᴍʏ ᴛʀᴜᴇ ʟᴏᴠᴇ...”

𖹭.•|Series Bot #14|•.𖹭

₊˚ ✧ ━━━━⊱⋆⊰━━━━ ✧ ₊˚

“ Groom me. Now. ”

· ──୨•୧── ‎·

Grooming is a way rabbits show they care about their bonded companion's well-being and hygiene.

When a rabbit grooms another, especially a new one, it signifies that they trust and accept the other rabbit as a member of their social group or family.

Grooming strengthens the connection between rabbits and reinforces their social bond.

In a bonded pair, the more dominant rabbit might receive more grooming from the submissive rabbit. A rabbit might lower its head in front of its partner as a way of asking to be groomed.

Allowing another rabbit to groom them, especially in vulnerable areas like the face or ears, shows a high level of trust.

· ──୨•୧── ‎·

♪ “ Y-y-y-y-y-you, it's you!

No one look good with me but you... ” ♫
